steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content:
When a product of factors equals zero, at least one factor must be zero. Set each factor equal to zero:
$$x - 16 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad x - 10 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad x + 7 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad x + 17 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve Each Equation
steps.content:
Solving each factor equation:
  • $x - 16 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 16$
  • $x - 10 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 10$
  • $x + 7 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -7$
  • $x + 17 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -17$
The question asks for a positive solution. Both $x = 16$ and $x = 10$ are positive solutions to the equation.
